title: Olympus consumer config (.olympus.json)
description: Per-repo policy for the Olympus mechanism. Every field is optional; omitted values fall back to a built-in default. This file is the ONLY place a consuming repo expresses repo-specific behavior — the Olympus scripts and workflows carry none.

default_branch.description: Branch the dev agent forks from and PRs target.

review_bot_login.description: GitHub login (or body-footer marker) of the review bot. Auto-merge / revise key off this to find its review.

dev_agent_name.description: Display name used in commit author + log lines for the implementer.

try.description: Triage adds this on verdict=do; starts the dev agent.

auto_agent.description: Marks dev-agent PRs eligible for the auto-merge / revise gates.

max_loc.description: Diff size ceiling for unattended auto-implementation.

contained_areas.description: Areas a change may touch and still be 'contained' (e.g. one package, docs/, one workflow).

test_hint.description: What a deterministic test looks like in this repo (e.g. 'cargo test', 'pytest').

language.description: Reply language. 'auto' = match the reporter; otherwise a fixed language code/name (e.g. 'en', 'zh').

build_cmd.description: The build + test command the dev agent must get green before opening a PR (e.g. 'make build && make test').

allow_network.description: Let the implement/revise agent use network commands (curl/wget/ssh/...). Default false: the claude harness denies direct network egress so a prompt-injected issue can't exfiltrate. Not a full sandbox — indirect egress needs OS-level isolation; see docs/security.md.

repo.description: owner/name the incident issue is filed on.

readiness.description: Optional deeper 'is it actually working' check. Omit for DOWN-only detection.

jq.description: jq filter over the health JSON yielding a readiness value.

expect.description: Value the jq filter must equal; anything else = parked.

model.description: Model name sent to the LiteLLM-style gateway.

harness.description: Which agent CLI drives the surfaces. Omit for the built-in claude (Claude Code) harness — that's back-compatible with every existing consumer.

kind.description: claude = built-in Claude Code. custom = run the 'command' template.

command.description: custom only: a shell command template invoked per agent run. Placeholders: {model} {prompt_file} {out} {tools} {write} {max_turns}. e.g. 'codex exec --model {model} --full-auto < {prompt_file} > {out}'.

model.description: Model id for this harness; overrides top-level .model. For a custom harness this is the {model} placeholder value.

health_probe.description: Poll the gateway's OpenAI-compatible /v1/models before each run (and to detect mid-run outages). Set false for harnesses whose endpoint isn't OpenAI-compatible.
